items.rationale: Launched a new Columbia research center focused on blockchain infrastructure, funded by the Ethereum Foundation; expresses unusually strong personal enthusiasm.

citations.quote: New research center focused on blockchain infrastructure @CUSEAS ! Made possible by @ethereumfndn. Super-excited about this, more to come!
Thank you Caspar, and I hope you'll spend some time with us at the center!

items.rationale: Co-authored a new foundational paper on DePIN, focused on the core problem of verifying that agreed-upon physical services/resources are actually being provided.

citations.quote: developing some of the foundations for DePIN (decentralized physical infrastructure) networks, and specifically the key problem of verifying

items.rationale: Ongoing line of work on which validators should be held accountable when a protocol stalls or violates consistency; a sustained, recent research thread.
items.age_note: Builds on 2024-05 EAAC/slashing work; restated in 2025-05, so current.

citations.quote: When a blockchain protocol stalls (i.e., has a liveness violation), which validators should be held accountable?

items.rationale: Enthusiastic about hard theory becoming the fastest zkVM in the world (Jolt), and optimistic that advanced cryptographic primitives will become practical over coming decades.
items.age_note: Last expressed 2024-12; not restated in 2025 corpus — older view, treat as persisted.

citations.quote: It's been amazing watching 102 pages of hard theory get turned into the fastest zkVM in the world.
i think they were expressing optimism that cryptographic primitives that seem even crazier than SNARKs could be made practical in the coming decades

items.rationale: Theory of shill-proof auctions explaining why Dutch auctions are popular in Web3; treats auction design as central to crypto markets.
items.age_note: Stated 2024-04; not restated since — older but core to his research identity.

citations.quote: Which auction formats are robust to shill bidding? Why are Dutch auctions so popular in Web3?

items.rationale: Produced tutorials and talks on automated market makers, loss-versus-rebalancing, and Tullock contests covering mining, staking, execution tickets, and shared sequencing.
items.age_note: Tutorials/talks from mid-to-late 2024; not refreshed in 2025 corpus.

citations.quote: Covers mining, staking, execution tickets, shared sequencing. Includes ongoing work with @PGarimidi @mikeneuder.

items.rationale: Built a framework to reason about restaking risks — whether validators can be safely reused across services without a small shock cascading into a catastrophic attack.
items.age_note: From 2024-08; not restated in 2025, but a substantive risk-analysis stance.

citations.quote: framework to reason about the risks of restaking. Key question: Under what conditions can validators be safely reused across multiple services?
When is a restaking network “robustly secure,” meaning that a small "shock"...cannot enable a catastrophic attack?

items.rationale: Proves slashing cannot achieve its intended deterrent purpose if message delays can't be bounded a priori, or in partially-synchronous/dynamically-available settings.
items.age_note: From 2024-05; foundational impossibility result, not contradicted later.

citations.quote: slashing in a proof-of-stake protocol cannot achieve its intended purpose if message delays cannot be bounded a priori

items.rationale: Shows typical longest-chain protocols (PoW like Bitcoin, PoS like Ouroboros) cannot offer non-trivial accountability (EAAC) guarantees against attackers.
items.age_note: From 2024-05; impossibility result, stands as stated.

citations.quote: This result rules out non-trivial EAAC guarantees for all typical longest-chain protocols (be they proof-of-work protocols like Bitcoin or proof-of-stake protocols such as Ouroboros)

items.rationale: Concerned that sellers can manipulate auction prices via shill bids, especially in public/observable Web3 auction settings; motivates shill-proof design.

citations.quote: A *shill bid* is a bid submitted by the seller in order to manipulate the selling price of an auction.

items.rationale: Not a token/price view. Inferred ecosystem alignment: his research repeatedly centers Ethereum, and his new center is Ethereum-Foundation-funded. No statement about ETH as an asset.
items.age_note: Inferred from 2025-09 EF-funded center and recurring Ethereum-focused research; never an explicit token view.

risk_regime.rationale: Roughgarden's statements are exclusively academic — auction theory, consensus/slashing impossibility results, restaking security, DePIN, zkVMs. He never expresses a market-timing, risk-on/risk-off, or asset-allocation posture.

notes: Tim Roughgarden is a researcher/theorist, not a markets commentator; the corpus contains essentially zero token-price or investment-posture statements, so tokens_excited/concerned and risk_regime are weakly supported by design. The strongest and MOST RECENT signal (2025-09) is genuine personal excitement about a new Ethereum-Foundation-funded blockchain-infrastructure research center and (2025-06) DePIN foundations — these are his current focus. Older 2024 threads (zkVMs/SNARKs, shill-proof auctions, slashing impossibility, restaking risk, LVR/MEV) are marked 'persisted' since they were stated once and not refreshed in 2025; none were reversed. His 'concerns' are theoretical impossibility/risk results (slashing limits, restaking cascades, longest-chain accountability), not market bearishness. The single ETH token entry is explicitly extrapolated from ecosystem alignment, not a stated asset view.
